key_insight: Scratch testing is a fast but imperfect way to assess adhesion and damage evolution in thermally sprayed coatings, and its results depend strongly on coating microstructure and test interpretation.
engineering_relevance: It helps engineers screen coating integrity and tribological performance across APS, HVOF, HVAF, D-gun, and related spray processes for aerospace, energy, automotive, and industrial use.
method: Correlate scratch response with microstructure, porosity, residual stress, and failure mode.
Use critical-load detection methods such as optical microscopy, acoustic emission, and multi-axis force measurement.
Compare outcomes across multiple thermal spray processes and coating systems.
evidence_signal: The paper is a critical review that consolidates findings from a wide range of thermally sprayed coatings and evaluates how different testing methods affect critical-load interpretation.
watch_item: Scratch-test results are not standardized and can vary significantly with methodology, so damage criteria and critical-load values should not be compared blindly across studies.
paper_title: A critical analysis of the scratch test for thermally sprayed coatings
